turns.content: I'm trying to parse a SQL query into my Rust program but I keep getting escape errors where backslashes are being interpreted literally instead of as escape characters. The error says 'invalid character in string literal' even though I just added quotes around the whole thing.

That sounds like you're fighting the wrong battle by trying to use a regular string when you need raw strings. Think of it like this: if you're reading a book with special symbols printed directly on the page, you don't want to re-read every word and try to interpret them yourself; you just want to copy the text exactly as it appears. Raw string literals in Rust are like that—they let you write code without having to manually escape backslashes or quotes inside the literal itself. When you use a regular string type, the compiler tries to parse every character, which is why your SQL query fails because of those unwanted escapes.
